inline_comments.message:
parsing xpath string in order to retrieve the request parameters 
should not be a responsibility of persistence service.
update interface to accept the ready to use parameters:

queryDataNodes(final String dataspaceName, final String anchorName, 
final String xpathPrefix, 
final Map<String, Object> queryLeaves,
final FetchDescendantsOption fetchDescendantsOption);

CpsPathException should be removed as out of scope for this task

replace with to_jsonb(:leavesAsJsonString)
no reason to initially limit the query with single leaf condition
update the method signature accordingly
cpsPath has been agreed a few month back to follow xPath functionality, so this user story is introducing teh first functionality for this. Niamh is taking the right approach

it is not in scope and it raises doubt/options about using and/or condition.
Stick to scope don't implement what is not needed now.
I think a more basic example might be more readable. ie bookstore or trees, birds & nests :-)

this will cause _any_ descendant with matching attribute being treated as a valid result.
if requested 'cpsPath' is /path[@attr=value]
and there is an entry with path /path/child/grand-child and {attr:value}
the entry of wrong type from wrong level of depth will be returned.
it's a bug

Both are right :), Can we encapsulate this on their own class e.g. CpsPath. Which will have this responsibility [line 136 - 140 + Excption if incorrect ], and will be passed as parameter for this method and others that will need it

I do not see reason why not use to_jsonb(:leavesAsJsonString), so I agree that is a good change to be done.
If there is code impact I will go with Toine,  my understanding is that there is no such code impact / change required by using it.
Can we reuse what is on line 19 or start bringing something in common for present tests and futures ones. So we don't need to add per use case data?
for now do not handle witDescendants option (as per original scope), it raises too many issues and questions

i asked Niamh the same before, and asked her to add tests to proof this is not the case. 
Maybe we need to double check those test again if they are passing for a different reason (false positives)
or do we need more scenarios.

i probably need to see it implemented to decide but I am always hesitant to go beyond the original scope, if it does work easily then we do need to clarify whether the filter will apply 'and' or 'or' but in either case this will NOT be used until we expand teh cpsPath functionality to handle multiple attributes with and/or as specified by xpath spec

I argued that before; we can have a good single data set that suits most use cases
(I stil wonder could we load the bookstore model and data from our tets resources somehow, maybe as part of a future improvement)

(re-supplying los t comment :-))
reword, sugestions:
return no result
return empty collection
find no data

had a closer look and I think we only addressed part of te problem I saw originally with a test. You  are right; the sql statement needs to be improved to prevent the scenario of further descendants having the same leaf also being returned.
I discussed with Niamh and provide some pointer how it could be solved. also that we need test (and test data) to clearly show and test the expected behavior
maybe CpsPathQueryDetails would be a beter name?
this name is specific for the current case, in future it would have many more options so I dont think we can have a methodname that 'reflect' the current attributes
depending on the method name you choose maybe use 'parse' here too?
create a Spec for the CPSPath (named will change) class itself, this test belongs there

I think original refers to a specific (older) test not sure of child any grandchild need same value
of child any grandchild need a leaf with same value just for you test name it accordingly
e.g commonLeafName commonLeafValue
if you DO need original for otehr test, I suggest you add an extra leaf where need for your test

often it is betert to NOT use possible java types in javdoc, just say 'the datanodes founds
I also would quantify it ie."0 or more Datanodes"

lost a comment update again :-(
I was suggesting
parse 
or createFromString

i think you misunderstood (and I seem to have lost a previous comment again) I think this needs a more generic name like 'parse' as the same method in the future wil cater for many different queries types with different parameters

can you add another scenario just to proof the functionality more:

incomplete xpath end:  '/parent-200/child-20[@common-leaf-name-int=5]'
no need for else block  (there is a return statement above)
doe this require some additional comments are we free to name the method what we want since it is hardcode natvie query anyway?
I alwyas prefer test ordered by basic positive scenarios first, then edge cases,, and then error scenarios...

Details? I will just call it  CpsPathQuery.
Also please use Builder pattern, CpsPathQuery should be immutable after "build" which now is called parse
can we move this to some descriptive method

agree it needs to be immutable but dont think the builder patern is needed, client will only ever provide one cpsPath string, the source for the query. 

we ran into many issues and discussions using builder pattern in separate class before. I would prefer something like fromString() or createFrom() I though 'parsing' was the appropriate verb anyway but am open to other sugegtsions.

Ideally the query parameters wouldn't need be exposed at all instead cpsPathQuery could create a native SQL query (string) as now is doen using annotation in teh FragmentReposity. I am not sure if there are alternatives for that. But let's not overengineer our first ever query in the final days before release! Typically I think these type of improvements should be done when we implement our 2nd query or even thirds....
as discussed need pattern to recognize integer form other possibilities like floats and Boolean (which are out of scope for this US)
at failign test for float values (should throw exception about not being supported yet)

I stand with what I said in my previous comment, at this stage it should be more than clear how to do the pattern and there are other classes as example if not. So lets don't block correct changes because of being afraid of new discussions. Request change is for a correct implementation not for an improvement.

so you want 2/3 classes?

CpsPath
CPsPathBuilder
 
and (n an existing) a 3rd class that parses the incoming string string and uses the builder to create a CPSPathQuery?!

or a builder that only has one with-Method (and never more)

CpsPathQuery = CpsPathBuilder.wihCpsPath(...).build()

it seems an inappropriate pattern to me but we can do if you insist
actually there is a few more (leagcy) tests in this class that miss this annotation if you get a chance can you add those please

expected output 

CpsPathBuilder.wihCpsPath(...).build()

where build will do the parse and return CpsPathQuery  with only getters [not setters], being CpsPathQuery immutable.

whether is done in one or 2 classes, I don't mind as long is done correctly.
Can we remove those @ constructors and force the use of createFrom
I think we can only do taht if we private setters...
@Setter(AccessLevel.PRIVATE) ? I will say we are doing it already somewhere
not applicable for you, is it
alwasy check if columns end up having same values always in each row, if they do, no need for that column :-)
cahneg to type see more detailed explaantion in next spec review
Probably OK to leve as upper case as you (have to) are referring to Java types but normally not needed

cps path is incomplete

OR

cps path misses the parent object
and a filter for a leaf of type #type
